name: Accounted as sheep for the slaughter
notes: In Romans 8:36 Paul cites Psalm 44:22 to show that present suffering for God's sake is no strange thing but the long-standing portion of His people. The psalmist laments that the faithful are killed all the day long and reckoned as sheep for the slaughter, even while they have not forgotten God's covenant. Paul takes up the same words, prefacing them with 'As it is written,' to assure believers that no affliction can separate them from the love of Christ. The shared core is the suffering remnant likened to slaughtered sheep, endured for the sake of God. The psalm reads 'counted' and Paul 'accounted'; this slight verbal variation in rendering the citation is left as a matter for further study.

links.label: For thy sake ... we are killed all the day long
